title: Trump as role model “really haunts me”
summary: Scott Galloway and Jessica Tarlov discuss the men Trump celebrated in the State of the Union address, and the women he left out, on Raging Moderates.
transcript_html:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7-VaPpnygqc

Transcript

Our country is winning again. In fact, we're winning so much that we really don't know what to do about it. Here with us tonight is a group of winners who just made the entire nation proud. The men's gold medal Olympic hockey team. Come on in. >> Jess, are you sick of winning? >> Not when it comes to hockey. >> I love hockey and I have some appreciation for it. Eight of the 12 golds at this Olympics were won by women. This guy just sends such a wrong message to young men. It comes back to

something that really haunts me when I think about it. And that is if if you're a 21-year-old male, since the age of 10, the dominant figure in your life as a role model has been President Trump. But this notion that somehow masculinity or that leadership involves diminishing women, I just think it's so destructive.
